entry.title: How to Configure and Perform Transport Checks in SAP CALM: SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Objective: This blog aims to guide users through the configuration and execution of transport checks in SAP Cloud ALM, with a specific focus on Downgrade Protection (DGP) and Cross-Reference Checks. These checks help ensure that transports do not unintentionally overwrite newer objects (DGP) and that all dependent or referenced objects are correctly included (Cross-Reference Checks), thereby maintaining system integrity and minimizing post-deployment issues in S/4HANA Private Cloud environments. This blog aims to guide users through the configuration and execution of transport checks in SAP Cloud ALM, with a specific focus on Downgrade Protection (DGP) and Cross-Reference Checks. These checks help ensure that transports do not unintentionally overwrite newer objects (DGP) and that all dependent or referenced objects are correctly included (Cross-Reference Checks), thereby maintaining system integrity and minimizing post-deployment issues in S/4HANA Private Cloud environments.

Transport checks is one of the new features that SAP CALM is providing.

The Transport Checks are available for Features application in Cloud ALM, and you can check a CTS-managed transport set to evaluate the impact on your production tenant.

The checks include Downgrade Protection and a Cross-Reference Check. It’s possible to choose to perform an individual check or both.

Downgrade Protection (DGP) tracks objects in transport requests, and reports conflicts when different versions of the same object that is saved in two or more transport requests is imported.

  • Predecessor Check (Sequence Violation)

The system can detect conflicting predecessors, that is, preceding transport requests containing conflicts, at the time of importing transport requests to the production.

  • Imminent Check (Downgrade Situation)

The system can detect impending (imminent) downgrade conflicts when transport requests are imported. This kind of conflict would become an actual downgrade if you ignored the conflict.

Cross-reference Check can detect inconsistencies for objects that are referenced in transport requests, indicating object dependencies.

Objects in transport requests sometimes refer to other objects that are not included in the transport. The cross-reference check applies a where-used-analysis to check the referenced objects in transport requests. If a referenced object doesn’t exist in the target system or if the referenced object in the source system has a different version than in the target system, the check indicates a potential error. In addition, the check shows the last transport requests for the missing object versions.

Let me take you through how to configure these transport checks and perform them in SAP CALM.
The first step is to enable the use case Transports: Check in Production system during the registration of the systems while configuring SAP CALM for Implementation:


To configure SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation for your SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, do check out my blog:
Re: How to Configure SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation for Your SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud Landscape

Just enabling Transports: check is not enough to perform the checks; RFC destination needs to be maintained with the source tenant, if you do not maintain it, you will get the below error message:

To maintain the RFC destination, follow the below steps:
Step 1: Goto SAP Note: 2475591 and download the role: SAP_SDF_ALM_TRCHK.SAP from the Attachments section:

Now goto the Development system of your landscape and upload the downloaded role in the tcode PFCG-> Role->Upload:

Step 2: Now Goto tcode SU01 in the Development system of your landscape and create a user, for example: TRCHECK_CALM, give the initial password and give the same role that we uploaded in step 1 to this user and save the User.

Step 3: Now we need to create RFC in Development system of the landscape, to do so goto SM59 and click on create button:

Give the Destination name (For example: TRCHECK_CALM_RFC) and select the connection type as 3:

In the Technical settings tab give the target host and instance no:

In the logon & Security tab, give the user we created in Step 2 and the password and save the RFC destination:

Do the connection test and authorization test:

Step 4: Login to Production system 000 client in SM30 Table view: /SDF/CMO_TARGET and maintain the RFC destination just created in step 3:

Once all the steps are performed, login to SAP CALM tenant and click on perform all checks in the Feature when the Transports have target tenant as Production system:

The results for Transports check are then reflected:

After the transport checks are performed, you can now deploy your changes to Production system.

entry.title: Keeping Up-to date with SAP Cloud ALM – What Features are released as per the roadmap?
entry.content:
Structure of this blog

This blog is structured to understand the following:

  • Manage Transformation Projects
  • New App: Guided Implementation
  • Change history for process authoring
  • Configuration library
  • Deployment scheduler for transport
  • Test plan copy
  • Mass upload of test cases
  • Mass configuration for events in the “health monitoring” application
  • Customer-defined KPIs in business process monitoring for non-ABAP-based components
  • Job & Automation Monitoring has new filters available
  • Health Monitoring now supports SAP Datasphere
  • Health Monitoring now supports  SAP Market Communication for Utilities
  • What's coming up?

 

Manage Transformation Projects

 Manage Transformation Projects app under Transformations section in Cloud ALM:

Create a transformation project based on available transformation scenarios. Currently only to make a selective data transition for SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) to the transformation functionality in SAP S/4HANA is available.

 

We can further assign a digital blueprint to the transformation project.

 

The transformation project in SAP Cloud ALM creates:

  • A project

 

  • Creates Tasks where the initial product for the “lean selective data transition” scenario has approximately 45 project steps from setup to cycle execution.

 

New App: Guided Implementation

To execute implementation projects by following the SAP Activate Methodology in the new Guided Implementation App.

 

It helps to:

  •  Get an overview of all the tasks in project

 

  • Change the status of a task

 

  •  Change the responsible persons for tasks

 

  • Change the planned completion dates for tasks

 

  • Check for available resources for tasks

 

  •  

Change history for process authoring

We can now view change history in Process Authoring. This offers transparency and accountability.

 

Configuration library

Configuration Library is now available in Cloud ALM. Under Configuration Library, all configuration unit originals and their elements from the configuration library folders are collected and displayed in a hierarchical structure.

 

Document relation to Solution Activity & Configuration Library

In the Documents app we can assign:

  • Solution activity library elements.

 

  • Configuration library elements

 

Deployment scheduler for transport

In the deployment app we can see all available deployment schedules:

 

                 Create a deployment plan in Manage Projects à Deployment Plan and assign the Releases and Systems Groups.

 

 

A deployment plan can include several system groups. When we can assign a deployment plan to the project, these system groups will also be assigned to the project as landscape.

We can deploy the Features as per the assigned Releases, Frequency, Target Tenant and feature status, which helps in scheduling transports.

 

Test plan copy

We can now create copies of test plans, to reuse them for subsequent testing cycles by multiple testers.

The copy of the test plan is created in the same project and with the same attributes as the source test plan.

 

Mass upload of test cases

We can now Upload multiple test cases in one file, which helps the tester in uploading multiple test cases at once, which was not possible earlier. It saves lot of time and makes test management much more efficient.

Upload of multiple test cases in one file to SAP Cloud ALM:

  • Click on the Test Preparation app
  • Navigate to the "Upload Button.
  • Download the template to fill it with your test cases

 

 

  • Upload the test cases.

Mass configuration for events in the “health monitoring” application

We can now configure mass events and assign Variants to multiple KPI’s at once.

 

 

 

Customer-defined KPIs in business process monitoring for non-ABAP-based components

In addition to the pre-defined KPI’s, we can add custom KPIs (up to 15 KPI’s as of now)

Currently this is focused on business technology platform and the architecture foundation for the data collection and instrumentation is based on open telemetry.

We can also monitor the app built on BTP. We can now create our own custom KPI’s to monitor the execution and report it in SAP cloud ALM.

Procedure

  • Choose Configuration to open the configuration panel.
  • Expand the sections àManaged Components KPI Management.
  • Choose àConfigure KPIs.
  • Choose à(Create Custom KPI.

 

  • Under Information, fill in the fields as below
  • Under Characteristics, define the characteristics of custom KPI and Save.

 

Activate the Data Collection for Custom KPIs

After the custom KPI has been created, we need to activate the data collection for the KPI in the managed service.

  • Choose Configuration to open the configuration panel.
  • Expand the sections Managed Components Services / Systems and make sure that the service from which the KPI should collect data is active.
  • Expand the KPI Management section to see a list of all KPIs that are available.
  • Select the custom KPI.
  • Go to the Data Collection tab.
  • Under Services / Systems, switch on the data collection for the relevant service.

 

Job & Automation Monitoring has new filters available

 In Analysis page, to further refine the jobs and automations displayed. Filters can be found under “Additional filters”, and they are:

  • Step/Executable: Filter by the step/executable name of the job or automation.
  • Executed By: Filter by the user who has executed the job or automation.
  • Scheduled By: Filter by the user who has scheduled the job or automation.

 

 

Intelligent Event Processing with Event Name

In Intelligent Event Processing, it’s now possible to view the Event Name and Service Type for the variant in the “Where Used” pane. Additionally, it’s now possible to use the “Live Search” to find variants using Event Name and Service Type information.

 

Health Monitoring now supports SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere is an integrated and persona-driven data warehouse-as-a-service solution that helps business and IT users better understand and analyze their business data.

We can configure KPI’s as below:

  • Memory Used by Spaces
  • Disk Used by Spaces
  • Disk Storage Used
  • Out-of-Memory Errors
  • Failed Tasks
  • Admission Control: Queue Events
  • Admission Control: Rejection Events

 

 

Health Monitoring now supports SAP Market Communication for Utilities

SAP Market Communication for Utilities application provides a cloud-based solution for the holistic management of communication processes in the energy market, tightly integrated with SAP for Utilities.

We can configure Certificate Validity KPI.

 

What's coming up?

  • ABAP Test Cockpit checks integration
  • Integrate the different check variants of the ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) configured on the managed system with SAP Cloud ALM
  • Enable a manual action to trigger the checks in ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) for the transports on feature level
  • Show the results of the checks in the feature details view accordingly for each transport
  • This will help in ensuring good quality of ABAP code.
  • Dashboard for RISE with SAP Methodology: operations view
  • Operations View, displaying the System Health of your selected systems, providing transparency about their operational health.
  • This shows the history and trend of the System Health score and its KPIs for selected systems over the last 30 days, allowing us to track progress over time, identify issues, and take appropriate actions to improve the System Health score accordingly
  • Support of test sequences in test plans
  • Define sequences in test plans for the execution of test cases
  • This will help the tester in managing the test case execution.
  • System availability events for SAP S/4HANA
  • To send a regular heartbeat metric to “health monitoring” through on-premises software like SAP S/4HANA or offerings like SAP Business Suite
  • Raise an event in health monitoring if the heartbeat metric is no longer received
  • Based on the event, configure different actions (for example, an alert or an email) to get notified if the system is no longer responding
  • Receive notifications if an on-premises system is no longer responding to SAP Cloud ALM

 

Please refer below link to request new features in Cloud ALM. This program is specifically aimed towards customers with productive licenses for SAP products and who would like to be able to suggest improvements in a continuously open channel.

Link to Influence portal: Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence

Please find the link on Roadmap explorer for more information on what is delivered and planned for upcoming quarters.

Link to Cloud ALM Roadmap Explorer: SAP Road Map Explorer 

I hope this blog helps you find all the required details on what’s new and upcoming on Cloud ALM.

entry.title: CRUD Operations in RAP ABAP in managed BO
entry.content:
1. Create a table what you want to do CRUD operations, or you can use any table of your choice

@EndUserText.label : 'product table'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zvb_product {
  key client     : abap.clnt not null;
  key product_id : abap.int4 not null;
  name           : abap.string(256);
  category       : abap.char(40);
  price          : abap.int4;
  currency       : abap.cuky;
  discount       : abap.int4;
}

2. Create a root interface view entity for the table You are going to do CRUD Operations.

 

 

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface View for Product'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity ZI_VBPRODUCT as select from zvb_product
{
  key product_id as Product_id,
  name as Name,
  category as Category,
  price as Price,
  currency as Currency,
  discount as Discount
}

3.   Create a projection view for the view entity created in step 2. And allow metadata extension annotation to be true.

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View product entity'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
@Search.searchable: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define root view entity ZI_VBPRODUCT_ENTITY
as projection on ZI_VBPRODUCT as product
{
   @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [
   { entity: {
               name: '/DMO/I_Customer',
               element: 'CustomerID'
       } }
   ]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
  key Product_id,
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [
   { entity: {
               name: '/DMO/I_Customer',
               element: 'FirstName'
       } }
   ]
  Name,
  Category,
  Price,
  Currency,
  Discount
}

Here I have used value help for some fields and searchable true annotation for searching.

4. Create a metadata extension for the Consumption view created in the step 3. Use at least single annotation for each field otherwise you may get an error if not you can continue.

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@UI: {
  headerInfo: {
    typeName: 'Product',
    typeNamePlural: 'Product',
    title: { type: #STANDARD , label: 'Product', value: 'product_id' }
  }
}
annotate view ZI_VBPRODUCT_ENTITY
    with
{
  .facet: [{ id: 'product_id', purpose: #STANDARD, type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE, label: 'product_id', position: 10 }]
  : { lineItem: [{ position: 10 },{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'Copy', dataAction: 'Copy' }] ,selectionField: [{ position: 20 }],
  identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'Product ID' }] }
    Product_id ;
    : { lineItem: [{ position: 20 }], identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Name' }], selectionField: [{ position: 30 }] }
    Name;
        : { lineItem: [{ position: 30 }], identification: [{ position: 30, label: 'Category' }],selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]}
    Category;
     : { lineItem: [{ position: 40 }], identification: [{ position: 40,label: 'Price' }],selectionField: [{ position: 50 }]}
    Price;
     : { lineItem: [{ position: 50}], identification: [{ position: 50, label: 'Currency' }],selectionField: [{ position: 60 }]}
    Currency;
    : { lineItem: [{ position: 60 }], identification: [{ position: 60, label: 'Discount' }],selectionField: [{ position: 70 }]}
    Discount;
}

5. Create the Behavior definition for the root view entity.  Make sure the mapping for the crud operations L.H.S will be the names of view and the R.H.S  names of table.

Note: L.H.S. and R.H.S names should not be same .

managed implementation in class zbp_i_vbproduct unique;
strict ( 2 ); //Uncomment this line in order to enable strict mode 2. The strict mode has two variants (strict(1), strict(2)) and is prerequisite to be future proof regarding syntax and to be able to release your BO.
define behavior for ZI_VBPRODUCT alias product // alias name can be as you like
persistent table zvb_product
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
//  field ( readonly ) product_id;
  mapping for zvb_product
{
  Product_id = product_id;
  Name       = name;
  Category   = category;
  Price      = price;
  Currency   = currency;
  Discount   = discount;
}
}

6.   Create the behavior definition for the consumption view entity.

7.    Create the class which is in the behavior definition of the root view entity. Just create and activate.

CLASS lhc_ZI_VBPRODUCT DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR zi_vbproduct RESULT result.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lhc_ZI_VBPRODUCT IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

8.   Create a service definition.

9.    Finally create a service binding and activate it. And click on preview to view the application .

FINAL OUTPUT

CREATE

UPDATE OR EDIT

DELETE

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - 用語入門
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、SAP Analytics Cloudのダッシュボードやレポートを作成する際に登場する基礎的な用語をご紹介します。

目次：

  • 「モデル」と「ストーリー」
  • 「メジャー」と「ディメンション」

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2025年6月に作成しています。）

「モデル」と「ストーリー」

SAP Analytics Cloudでダッシュボード・レポートを作成する際、データの定義を行ったり、データを格納する箱となるデータモデルと、チャートやテーブルなどを使用してデータを表現または分析するフロント部分となるストーリーがセットで必要になります。

モデル

モデルでは、ストーリーで可視化させる準備として、データのモデル構造の定義（メジャー・ディメンション）や計算項目の追加、各ディメンションのタイプや階層・プロパティ・緯度／経度の情報の定義を行うことができます。

またモデル以外に、よりアドホックにデータの準備を行うことができるデータセットがあります。
モデルとデータセットの違いや、詳細についてはヘルプページをご覧ください：データセットまたはモデルのどちらかを選択 | SAP Help Portal

ストーリー

ストーリーでは、様々なチャート（棒グラフ、円グラフ、バブルチャートなど）やテーブル、地図などでデータを表現することができます。ストーリーの中にページを複数作成し、目的別のダッシュボード・レポートを作成します。一つのストーリー／ページに複数のモデルからデータを表示することが可能なため、様々なデータを参照・分析するダッシュボード・レポートを作成することができます。

ストーリーのページには、「レスポンシブ」ページと「キャンバス」ページの2種類があります。
レスポンシブは、さまざまな画面サイズで表示するときに自動的にリサイズされるレイアウトを作成できます。ストーリーをデスクトップブラウザだけでなく、モバイルアプリでも使用予定の場合はレスポンシブで作成することを推奨します。
キャンバスは、チャート、テーブルなどのオブジェクトをピクセル単位で配置することができます。デスクトップブラウザのみでの使用で、カチッとしたストーリーを作成したい場合はキャンバスで作成することを推奨します。
ストーリを新しく作成する際に、レスポンシブまたはキャンバスを選択して作成することになりますが、一つのストーリーにレスポンシブとキャンバスページを混在させることも可能です。


「メジャー」と「ディメンション」

SAP Analytics Cloudでは、データの各項目をメジャーもしくはディメンションのどちらかに分けて管理します。売上高・売上原価のような金額や件数、従業員数などの数値項目をメジャーと呼び、地域や品目、事業領域など分析軸となる項目をディメンションと呼びます。

メジャーとディメンションは、チャートやテーブルの作成の際に使用します。例えば、以下のように製品カテゴリ―別の売上を確認するようなチャートを作成したい場合、メジャーを「売上」、ディメンションを「製品カテゴリー」とすることで作成することができます。

メジャー

メジャーでは、金額だけでなく人数や件数なども扱うことができます。金額の場合、JPYやUSDなど通貨タイプを設定でき、金額以外の場合でも、単位（%やkgなど）を自由に追加することができます。値の集計方法を合計だけではく、平均や件数、最大などに、メジャーごとに設定することができます。

ディメンション

ディメンションのタイプは、以下の5つがあります。

ディメンションタイプ用途
バージョン実績、予算、計画、予測などのカテゴリに分けて管理
勘定売上高、売上原価、販管費、純利益、現金、買掛金などの勘定科目を管理
階層、式、通貨、単位などの定義が可能
日付時間的な要素
年、半期、四半期、月、日など複数の粒度で管理される
一般あらゆる種類のビジネスデータを分類・分析するために汎用的に使用可能
プロパティとして説明、親子階層、通貨、緯度・経度などの定義が可能
組織組織構造を管理
組織の責任者や通貨属性なども管理可能

メジャーとディメンションの詳細についてはヘルプページをご覧ください：ディメンションとメジャーについて | SAP Help Portal

以上、基礎的な用語のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。
はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Community からご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: po line item text is not visible in Ledger line item text

entry.title: SAP SOAP Sender & Receiver Adapters: Your Integration Game Changers
entry.content:
SOAP Sender and Receiver Adapters in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI)

Ever felt lost in the maze of SAP integration options? You’re not alone! I’m Rishika Sairi, an SAP Integration Specialist passionate about making complex topics simple. In this blog, I’ll show you how to use the SOAP Sender Adapter in SAP CPI to connect inbound SOAP requests with an OData receiver—step by step and hands-on.

If you find this guide helpful, drop a comment or give a thumbs up—your kudos keep me motivated to share more!

 

 Use Case Overview

We want to expose a SOAP service that:

  1. Accepts an order number via a SOAP request.
  2. Fetches the shipment date using an OData service.
  3. Responds back with the enriched order response.

Why use SOAP instead of REST, OData, or GraphQL?

  • SOAP is chosen for strict data contracts, strong security, and reliable messaging—ideal for enterprise and SAP integration. It uses XML and works well for complex operations and when both sender and receiver must agree on message formats. REST and GraphQL are more flexible and modern, but SOAP’s structure is better for scenarios needing formal agreements and type safety

WSDL and Its Role in SOAP

  • WSDL (Web Services Description Language) is an XML file that describes what a SOAP service can do: available operations, input/output formats, and where to access it.
  • The WSDL acts as a contract. Both sender and receiver use it to generate code and ensure messages match exactly, reducing errors and making integration predictable.

 

Summary Table

Feature

SOAP (with WSDL)

REST/OData/GraphQL

Contract

Strict (WSDL)

Flexible/Schema-based

Security

Built-in (WS-Security)

Custom/simple

Data Format

XML

JSON/XML

Complexity

Higher

Lower

 

SOAP + WSDL = strong contract, type safety, and predictable integration—best for SAP scenarios needing reliability and clear structure

Step-by-Step iFlow Design

Step 1. Start a New Integration Flow Project

  • Open SAP Integration Suite → Design → Create an Integration Flow.
  • Name: SOAP_sender_Adapter.

Step 2. Configure the SOAP Sender Adapter

  • Drag the SOAP sender from the Sender section.

 

 

Field

Value used

Explanation / What You Need to Do

Address*

/SoapUIdemo

The endpoint path for the SOAP sender. Clients will call the service at https://<tenant_url>/cxf/SoapUIdemo.

Service Definition

WSDL

Indicates the service is defined via a WSDL file.

Use WS-Addressing

Unchecked

Leave unchecked unless the client requires WS-Addressing.

URL to WSDL*

/wsdl/GetOrderShipmentDetails.wsdl

Relative path to the uploaded WSDL file within the CPI tenant. Make sure this WSDL is uploaded under the "Resources" section of the iFlow.

Service

p2:GetOrderShipDetailsService

The service name defined in the WSDL (<wsdl:service> tag). Must match the WSDL exactly.

Endpoint

p2:GetOrderShipDetailsServiceSoap

The port/endpoint name from the WSDL (<wsdl:port> inside the <service>). Also must match exactly.

Processing Settings

Robust

Recommended setting for reliable SOAP communication. Ensures message persistence and retries in case of failure.

Authorization

User Role

Indicates that clients must authenticate using a role-based access policy.

User Role

ESBMessaging.send

CPI's default role for sending messages. The calling user (e.g., a technical user) must be assigned this role in the SAP BTP cockpit.

 

What You Need to Configure

  1. WSDL File Upload
    • Go to your iFlow → Resources → WSDL.
    • Upload the file named GetOrderShipmentDetails.wsdl.
    • Get the wsdl file from this drive [ https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/14OdkKwHEkMeH_Jdfvamj4LaAWvgNlP9x?usp=sharing ] 
    • Ensure the file path matches /wsdl/GetOrderShipmentDetails.wsdl.
  2. Correct Service and Endpoint Names
    • <wsdl:service name="GetOrderShipDetailsService">
    • <wsdl:port name="GetOrderShipDetailsServiceSoap">
    • Open the WSDL and verify:
    • The namespace prefix p2: should also match the targetNamespace in the WSDL.
  3. Security Role Assignment
    • Assign the role ESBMessaging.send to the user/service instance that will invoke this SOAP service.
    • Use Basic Authentication or OAuth as required.
    • In SAP BTP Cockpit:
  4. Endpoint URL for Clients
    • Your external client can access the service via: https://<your-cpi-tenant>/cxf/SoapUIdemo
    • Make sure this is reachable from the client environment (firewall, IP allowlist, etc.).

 

 

Step 3. Add a Content Modifier to extract and store the Order Number from the SOAP payload.

 

Field

Value

Explanation

Action

Create

Creates a new message header with the name specified. If the header already exists, it will overwrite it.

Name

OrderNumber

Name of the message header being created. It will be available throughout the iFlow.

Source Type

XPath

Extracts the value using an XPath expression from the incoming XML payload.

Source Value

//OrderNumber

The XPath expression pointing to the OrderNumber element in the request payload.

Data Type

java.lang.String

The expected data type of the header value.

 

 

Step 4: Using OData

  • Add a Request-Reply step to call the OData backend.
  • Drag and connect an OData Adapter to a Receiver.

 

Why Am I using Northwind Service:

I chose the Northwind service because it offers ready-to-use sample order data with no authentication required, follows standard OData conventions for easy querying, and is well-documented, making it perfect for quick testing and integration scenarios

 

Field

Value

Explanation

Address

https://services.odata.org/NorthWind/Northwind.svc

The base URL of the OData service endpoint.

Proxy Type

Internet

Connects over the internet. Choose "On-Premise" if using a Cloud Connector.

Authentication

None

This public service does not require authentication. Use "Basic" or "OAuth2" if needed.

CSRF Protected

Unchecked

Not needed for GET requests; CSRF is typically used with POST, PUT, DELETE.

Reuse Connection

Checked

Improves performance by reusing HTTP connections.

 

 

Field

Value

Explanation

Operation Details

Query (GET)

Fetch data using a GET request.

Resource Path

Orders

Target entity set from the OData service.

Query Options

$select=OrderID,CustomerID,...

Only selected fields are fetched for better performance. Complete list: OrderID, CustomerID, EmployeeID, OrderDate, RequiredDate, ShippedDate, ShipVia, Freight, ShipName, ShipAddress, ShipCity, ShipRegion, ShipPostalCode, ShipCountry.

Enable Batch Processing

Unchecked

Not needed for single queries. Enable for multiple operations.

Custom Query Options

Empty

Can be used for filters or $expand if needed.

Content Type

Atom

XML-based format. Change to JSON if preferred and supported by target service.

Page Size

Empty

Optional. Used for pagination in large result sets.

 

 

  • The Connection Source has two options, one is Remote and the other one is Local EDMX file. As the NorthWindService doesn’t have any kind of authentication to clear I have used up the Remote one.
  • The operation is set to Query (GET) for the order details
  • Entity – Orders, we must select the fields what we needed according to requirement. I have selected all the fields.

 

 

 

  • Verify the Query after selecting fields, If any changes need to be in the query we must modify in the step 3.
  • Click Finish.

 

Step 5: Mapping the Response

  • Add a Message Mapping step to map the OData response to the final SOAP response structure.
  • Input: OData format.
  • Output: SOAP structure.

Why Is Mapping Needed?

  1. OData responses are typically in JSON or XML with a different format than the SOAP response.
  2. Mapping helps reshape this data into the required SOAP structure.
  • After the SOAP request is received, the OData call fetches additional data (e.g., shipment date) related to the order.
  • The final SOAP response must follow a specific schema or structure defined by the SOAP service (WSDL/XSD).

NOTE: Here in the mapping the 1:1 mapping are always mandatory. I have taken the reference of the names from both sides and matched them accordingly.

Step 6: Add End Event

  • Place an End Message after the mapping to complete the flow.

Step 7: Deploy the Integration flow and Note the URL (Needs to send to the receiver).

SOAP API EXECUTION USING SOAP UI:

  • You can download the Soap UI from here https://www.soapui.org/
  • Create a new soap project and import the necessary WSDL File 

 

 

 

 

  • Place the SOAP API URL in the designated place
  • Set the Auth to basic and give me the SOAP API tenants Integration flow service key credentials
  • Send the request by tapping the play symbol along with filling the OrderNumber to be 10250[My reference].

SOAP Receiver Adapter

In this, we'll walk through designing an iFlow where a client sends an HTTPS request (in JSON), and the integration flow:

  • Converts it to XML,
  • Maps the data,
  • Sends it to a SOAP backend via SOAP Receiver Adapter,
  • And returns the response.

RESONS FOR SECOND IFLOW

  • We are consuming the SOAP Service that is in the first integration flow like exploring the SOAP Receiver Adapter
  • Instead of using the SOAP UI for sending the request we are using the REST API thorough HTTP to consume the Soap API Service.

 Use Case Overview

An external application (HTTPS client) sends a JSON request. The iFlow:

  1. Converts JSON to XML.
  2. Maps the message to match the SOAP backend schema.
  3. Sends the message to a backend system using the SOAP receiver.
  4. Returns the backend response to the client.

Step-by-Step iFlow Design

Step1: Create a New Integration Flow

  • Go to SAP Integration Suite → Design → Create iFlow.
  • Name: Soap_Receiver_Adapter.

 

Step 2: Configure HTTPS Sender

  • Add an HTTPS sender adapter.
  • Configure the Address (e.g., /Receiveradapterr).
  • Authorization- UserRole>ESBMessaging.send

Step 3: Convert JSON to XML

  • Use a JSON to XML Converter step.
  • This is required because SOAP backends typically consume XML, not JSON.  

 

  1. Message Mapping
  • Add a Message Mapping step to convert incoming XML structure to the format expected by the SOAP backend.

 

Source: Accepts XSD. Convert the xml payload that you have sent in postman.

Simulation:

  • The sample payload in this case is
    <?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><root><productIdentifier>HT-2000</productIdentifier></root>
  • This is the XML file that is used after adding the root element to the payload that you are sending through the postman

 

Connection Details:

  • Address: The service endpoint URL is: https://5b61c35atrial-it-cpitrial05-rt.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/cxf/SoapUIdemo
    This is the actual URL where the SOAP request will be sent(from the sender).
  • Proxy Type:
    Set to Internet, which means the connection is routed through the CPI internet proxy.
  • URL to WSDL:
    /wsdl/GetOrderShipmentDetails.wsdl
    This is the relative path to the WSDL file used to define the SOAP service.
  • Service, Endpoint, and Operation Name:
    These are parsed from the WSDL:
    • Service: p1:GetOrderShipDetailsService
    • Endpoint: p1:GetOrderShipDetailsServiceSoap
    • Operation Name: p1:GetOrderShipmentDetails
      This operation will be triggered when the message is sent.
  • Authentication:
    Set to Basic with the Credential Name as Servicekey.
    CPI will look up a credential entry with this name (maintained in the Security Material) and use its username/password.
  • Timeout (in ms):
    Set to 60000, meaning the integration flow will wait up to 60 seconds for a response.
  • Keep-Alive:
    Enabled. This allows the connection to be reused, improving performance on multiple requests.

WS-Security Configuration (SOAP Receiver Adapter)

  1. None – No WS-Security headers added. Use when not required by the service.
  2. Based on Policies in WSDL – Auto-applies security from WSDL-defined policies.
  3. Via Manual Configuration in Channel – Add custom WS-Security headers manually (e.g., UsernameToken, Signature).

Use based on target service’s security needs.

Deploy the Integration Flow and monitor it in the Trace mode.

Trigger the HTTP endpoint with the appropriate payload using postman and generate token using service key from integration flow instance.

Disclaimer:
All the views and opinions in this blog are my own and are made in my personal capacity. SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: Understanding Joule - (Step #8) Joule for Consultants
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Understanding Joule in 8 Baby Steps Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

#4 Joule Interaction Patterns

#5 Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

#6 Document Grounding in Joule

#7 Joule for Developers

In this blog, we will learn about Joule for Consultants.

 

What is SAP Joule for Consultants?

SAP Joule for Consultants is an AI-powered assistant, which is designed to enhance the efficiency of consultants working on SAP projects.

It capability leverages the most up-to-date and comprehensive knowledge base to deliver concise and accurate answers for various SAP topics.

Unlike other flavours of Joule which are embedded inside SAP solutions, SAP Joule for Consultants is a SaaS offering and can be accessed directly from a browser.

 

How to access Joule for Consultants

Once customers buy the license for Joule for Consultants, they are provided it as a SaaS offering. Users can then access the Joule for Consultants using the dedicated URL provided by their organization.

Below image shows the landing page. Just start a conversation by typing your question in the input field.

 

 

Here are some of the example questions you can ask to Joule for Consultants:

  • What are the key configuration steps to activate India GST in SAP S/4HANA?
  • How to create a subaccount in SAP BTP?
  • Generate ABAP code for a sales price calculation BAdI that decreases the location-specific price by 10% for every 2 degrees above 20 Celsius.
  • Generate a sample configuration guide for setting up a new company code.
  • How can I optimize freight costs using advanced algorithms and real-time data in SAP Transportation Management?

Below image shows a response from Joule for Consultants.

 

Who are the targeted users for SAP Joule for Consultants?

SAP consultants working on various tasks such as code analysis, documentation lookup, troubleshooting, implementation etc. can use this Joule to reduce their effort.

SAP projects involve multiple systems, complex business processes, and hundreds of configurations and code artifacts. Traditionally, consultants spend a significant amount of time in:

  • Searching through SAP Help, Notes, and documentation
  • Interpreting ABAP code written by others
  • Debugging system issues without clear direction
  • Repeating tasks manually across systems

 

With SAP Joule for Consultants, they can simply ask their questions in plain English and get curated, accurate and context-aware response. It will save lot of time and effort.

 

How is SAP Joule for Consultants better that other AI assistants, such as ChatGPT?

General AI tools such as ChatGPT are very powerful for broad knowledge retrieval and conversational AI. However, they have some major issues in the context of SAP knowledge, for example:

  • They have access to only publicly available information.
  • They lack accurate, real-time access to latest content from SAP. Due to which they might provide outdated or generic responses.
  • They source information from various non-official and community-based inputs, making it less reliable for enterprise SAP consulting.
  • They operate as a standalone AI and cannot interact with SAP directly. This limits their effectiveness.
  • They lack enterprise-specific security features. This means organizations are responsible for putting the necessary safeguards in place.

 

SAP Joule for Consultants is designed to solve all these problems. Let’s see how!

 

Comprehensive Knowledge Base

SAP Joule for Consultants is built upon:

  • 200,000+ pages of SAP documentation and learning content
  • 50+ SAP Certifications
  • 2+ terabytes of expertly curated SAP Communities content

This vast knowledge base equates to knowledge that would typically require a decade of consultant training.

 

Largest SAP-specific code repository

SAP Joule for Consultants has access to:

  • 250 million lines of ABAP code
  • 30 million lines of CDS code
  • Concise summary of code purpose, logic, and structure

Therefore, it can provide explanations and guidance on ABAP code to help consultants understand code purpose, logic, and structure and many more.

 

Up-to-Date Information and High Precision

SAP Joule for Consultants delivers responses grounded in official SAP documentation and verified resources. It ensures the reliability and alignment with SAP standards.

It also is continuously updated with the latest SAP releases, best practices, and security updates. Hence can provide accurate response on latest topics.

SAP Joule for Consultants is aligned with SAP security, compliance, and governance guidelines.

 

How does SAP Joule for Consultants work?

SAP Joule for Consultants uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to provide accurate and relevant answers. RAG combines two powerful techniques to ensure the information is both smart and up to date. First, it retrieves information from a large database or collection of documents to find the most relevant details related to the question. Then, it generates a detailed and accurate answer using the information found.

This way, SAP Joule for Consultants ensures that the answers are based on the latest and most reliable information available.

 

Hope by now, you have got a clear understanding on Joule for Consultants. For more information, you may refer to

  • SAP Joule for Consultants
  • SAP Joule for Consultants Help
  • SAP Joule for Consultants Pricing and Product details
  • KPMG and SAP Joule for Consultants: Pioneering the Future of AI-Driven Consulting
  • SAP Consulting with Joule Demo

Is the process of using IAS as a proxy for CDC and Entra a valid solution or do things get too bespoke / messy if introducing more than the single Entra IDP?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Ariba Supplier Management
entry.content:
Key Features of SAP Ariba Supplier Management

  1. Supplier Lifecycle Management: SAP Ariba simplifies supplier onboarding and qualification processes, reducing cycle times and ensuring that supplier data is accurate and up-to-date. This is achieved through automated workflows and integration with SAP ERP systems 

  2. Performance Management: The platform provides tools for continuous monitoring and evaluation of supplier performance. Businesses can set performance metrics and track compliance, helping to identify and mitigate risks proactively 

  3. Risk Management: SAP Ariba includes robust risk management features, such as risk due diligence and proactive risk monitoring. This helps businesses protect their brand reputation and ensure compliance with regulations like the German Supply Chain Due Diligence Act 

  4. Sustainability and ESG Compliance: The solution supports sustainability initiatives by enabling businesses to assess and manage suppliers based on environmental, social, and governance (ESG) criteria. This ensures that procurement decisions align with corporate sustainability goals 

  5. Integration with Procurement Apps: SAP Ariba integrates seamlessly with other procurement solutions, maintaining consistent supplier qualification and segmentation. This integration helps businesses drive spend to preferred suppliers and reduce risks at every step of the procurement process 

Benefits of Using SAP Ariba Supplier Management

  • Improved Collaboration: By centralizing supplier information and processes, SAP Ariba enhances collaboration between buyers and suppliers, leading to better communication and more efficient procurement operations 
  • Enhanced Compliance: Automated workflows and comprehensive risk management tools ensure that businesses remain compliant with industry regulations and standards 
  • Cost Savings: Streamlined processes and improved supplier performance management contribute to significant cost savings and operational efficiencies 
  • Greater Transparency: The platform provides greater visibility into supplier activities and performance, enabling businesses to make informed decisions and manage risks effectively 

Conclusion

SAP Ariba Supplier Management is a powerful tool that helps businesses manage their supplier relationships more effectively. By leveraging its comprehensive features, companies can enhance their procurement processes, reduce risks, and achieve their sustainability goals. As the business landscape continues to evolve,

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Time Conversion and Material Cost Ca...
entry.content:
Hello Mithundogra,

first of all, I'm assuming you are using the "Create Material Cost Estimate" app (CK11N) in S/4HANA Public Cloud, I hope this is correct.

In various steps during the costing process, a rounding might happen for the quantity and value fields. Typically a rounding down to zero means that there was only a very small amount for the activity to begin with. Usually our recommendation in those cases is to adjust the costing lot size for example to 100 or 1000, depending on the products value and the quantities of materials and time used.

If this is not a suitable solution for you and if you can provide me with a specific example, I can take another look at it. Please provide me the activity price/cost rate, the routing and the cost estimate of your example. Thank you.

Best regards
Marvin

entry.title: Re: Control and Command keys not swapped in SAP GUI for Java
entry.content:
Dear @fabianlupa 

Regarding customizing shortcuts in SAP GUI for Java, unfortunately this is not possible, as many of these shortcuts are set by the backend depending on the ABAP application from a given set in the menu painter. And overwriting Control for Command on the frontend side would cause conflicts with other already existing shortcuts provided by SAP GUI.

I understand your frustration regarding this topic, but unfortunately there is nothing we can do at frontend side as this is handled by the backend.

If you need further assistance with shortcuts I recommend you access the documentation below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap_gui_for_java/8cb7be1dd06242f48bcd643e33bbf31e/373cd67490b040d4b02e7f1a7047185f.html 

Best Regards,
Guilherme

entry.title: Re: How to setup Time Evaluation in Time Tracking
entry.content:
Hello, 

this is amazing article. Thank you for sharing this. Also in comments I found lot of valuable questions and answers.

I'd like to know your opinion, if these scenarios are possible to fulfill with Time Tracking license and current tools.

1. Scenario is Clock Time Positive - If employee clocked in and out multiple times and made 3 records of meal breaks during day, each with same time type. Would it be possible to valuate each record separately one by one and check breaks duration? And If any of these breaks won't last more than 30 minutes? I tried couple of ways, but as all these record were made on same day with same time type, system is aggregating all these breaks into 1 record. If each break lasted 15 minutes, as system aggregate all records into one, system thinks it lasted 45 minutes (3 x 15 mins).  Filter Single Record didn't work properly. Will appreciate any advice please. Thank you.

 

2. Scenario: is it possible to pull unpaired time events from SF somehow please? I found API GET /timeevents where are fields like "pairingStatusCode" which I don't know what contains. Are these single Clocks In stored somewhere where it is possible to pull it with Export, or Integration Center? Reason why we're looking for this is, that if employee worked more than 5 hours, we'd like to raise alert message to employee and manager that they should take a Meal Break. But as Clock in is unpaired time event I can't calculate time from Clock In till "now".

Thank you all in advance for any tips and advices.

Lukas

entry.title: Two-Tier ERP: SAP IBP integration with Subsidiary-HQ model
entry.content:
SAP’s Two-Tier ERP strategy with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers headquarter-subsidiary, central services, supply chain ecosystem deployment models enabling business and IT to work together to build sustainable, scalable solutions providing all benefits of cloud deployment – agility, standardization and low total cost of ownership (TCO) – while maximizing existing investments in their ERP systems.

The two-tier model can be adopted by businesses who have already deployed tier 1 on their existing systems like the ECC or SAP S4/HANA private cloud and looking to deploy the 2nd tier to S4/HANA public cloud.

 

 

Business Scenario:

Integrated Business planning (IBP) on a two-tier model can specifically be applied in scenarios where the headquarters (HQ) is responsible for overall demand aggregation and global supply chain strategy, while regional subsidiaries handle local sales forecasts based on market conditions. The use case for this could be with industries such as consumer electronics, automotive parts, or pharmaceuticals with regional demand variations or FMCG companies where HQ maintains planning integrity, but subsidiaries influence short-term demand signals.

Process Steps:

One of the key criteria for a simple demand planning scenario in the 2-tier setup is the master data and transactional data sync with IBP.

 

Detailed steps are as follows:

  1. Integrate master data such as product, location and customer master from the Headquarters (Private cloud) to IBP using SAP Cloud Platform Integration for data services (SAP CI-DS)
  2. Integrate transactional data such as the historical sales order from both the public and private clouds to IBP using iflows (CI) and CI-DS respectively.
  3. The delta of two sales order numbers (Actuals sales quantity) is then used to run the forecasting algorithm in IBP.
  4. IBP captures the total demand quantity, which is sent back as Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs) to the Headquarters (Private cloud) for generation of production orders.

Business Benefits:

  • Local teams add market intelligence to statistically driven HQ forecasts, resulting in better demand signal accuracy.
  • SAP IBP ensures a harmonized demand planning process, reducing inconsistencies between regions.
  • HQ and subsidiaries can collaborate in real time on the same planning data, enabling quick alignment.
  • HQ can own macro-level forecasting and strategy, while subsidiaries focus on operational/tactical forecast adjustments—reducing micromanagement and empowering local teams.
  • Clear roles and responsibilities improve data quality and accountability, as the model defines ownership of planning at each organizational level.
  • Workflow and collaboration features in SAP IBP (e.g., Excel add-in, task management) allow faster alignment between HQ and subsidiaries on consensus forecast.

Integration Setup:

Reference links:

SAP ERP and SAP IBP technical integration using CI-DS.

How to use SAP Cloud Integration for Data Services (CI-DS) to load data from S/4HANA with Generic Extractor – Part 1

How to use SAP Cloud Integration for Data Services (CI-DS) to load data from S/4HANA with Generic Extractor – Part 2

Getting Started with iFlows in SAP Integration Sui... - SAP Community

Overview | SAP IBP - Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Conclusion:

Product manufactured at the factory location or the headquarters for their subsidiaries, calls for end to end visibility of planning and timely execution of supply chain. Based on the demand forecasting/planning at the subsidiary side, manufacturer at headquarters can generate right production orders to prevent over/under supply scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Process Variant
entry.content:
Hello Prasad,

Onboarding tasks such as: RNHD , PDC , CDC , Compliance, E-signature, primary and secondary new hire tasks, are assigned based on the Process Variant.

If the steps to be taken from the onbordees are the same, and in the same order for all onbordees globally, then they might not benefit from having different Process Variants.

If onbordees from different countries are not required to complete the same steps, then different Process Variants will be required.
Process Variant Manager

Kind Regards,
Noemi

entry.title: Re: Where the email showing up on invoice coming from?
entry.content:
Resolution

For adding the address to the company code, please use SSCUI 105651 - Edit Addresses for Company Codes.

Please follow the steps below:

  1. Go to the CBC Workspace
  2. Go to Configuration Activities;
  3. Open SSCUI 105651 - Edit Addresses for Company Codes
  4. Select 2nd company code by clicking the checkbox;
  5. Click the address button at the top.

SAP Help Portal: Setting Up Organizational Structure 

Please note that once an organizational unit is created, only its name and address are changeable. It cannot be deleted or unassigned.

entry.title: 🚀 Change Project in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 (V2)
entry.content:
🚀 How to Execute a Change Project in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 (V2): Transporting Configuration Between Test and Production

With the release of SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 (V2), SAP continues to modernize its CRM landscape, delivering a clean, scalable, and intuitive user experience. One critical area of focus for implementation teams is the ability to transport configurations safely and effectively between environments—especially from Test (Quality Assurance) to Production.
In this blog, we’ll guide you through how to execute a Change Project in SAP Sales Cloud V2, providing a step-by-step walkthrough, important considerations, and best practices for a successful transport process.

🔧What Is a Change Project in SAP Sales Cloud V2?
A Change Project in SAP Sales Cloud V2 is a structured method for transporting configuration changes between systems. It supports a multi-tier architecture, allowing you to define one source system and multiple target systemsfor a given customer ID.
Think of it as a container for tested changes—ensuring only validated configurations are promoted to production.

✅Pre-requisites
Before starting your transport, ensure the following:
1. Both Test and Production systems are maintained under
Settings > All Settings > Change Project > Administration
2. You have administrator rights in both systems.

🛠️ Step-by-Step Guide: Executing a Change Project

🔹Step 1: Set Target System
In the Test (source) system, navigate to: Settings > All Settings > Change Project > Administration
Set the System Type of your Production system to "Target" in the system settings table.



💡Note: When you initiate the Initial Load, it captures all current changes in a service before the Change Project is created. This step must be repeated for each individual service.
Currently supported services:
• Extension
• Mashup

⚠️Known Limitations (Extensibility Service)
The Extensibility Administration includes Custom Fields, Field Attributes, and Data Privacy, but currently does notsupport:
• Custom Logic (Dynamic Properties, Validations, Determinations, External Hooks)
• Assignment Rules
• Custom Logic Administration
• Custom Code Lists / Restrictions
• Custom Services
• Workspace Sequence
• Language Adaptation

⚠️Special Case: Mashup Service
When transporting a mashup, the URL field is not transported and stays empty in the target system. As a result, users may see a warning message.
To resolve this:
• Manually enter the URL in the Production system.
• To enable this feature long-term, create a support case with component CEC-CRM-PM-PF and the subject:
“Enable URL Editing for Transported Mashups”.

🔹Step 2: Start Initial Load (Extensibility Service)
Once the target system is configured, initiate the Initial Load for the Extensibility Service.
⚠️Ignore the Change Project status for now—continue to the next steps.

🔹Step 3: Publish Change Project
In the Test (source) system, navigate to:
Settings > All Settings > Change Project > Change Project > Publish Change Project

🔹Step 4: Transport Change Project
Still in the Test system, go to:
Settings > All Settings > Change Project > Change Project > Transport

🔹Step 5: Activate in Target System
In the Production (target) system:
Settings > All Settings > Change Project > Change Project > Activate Change Project

🎉Congratulations! You have successfully executed a Change Project in SAP Sales Cloud V2 and transported your configuration to the Production system.

🔄Stay Up to Date
Just like any software product, SAP Sales Cloud V2 is continuously evolving. Additional services and enhancementsare on the roadmap. To stay current with new capabilities, regularly check the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

entry.title: Important lessons from SAP SAPPHIRE Orlando 2025(Virtual) focusing Data, AI & Sustainability
entry.content:
Thank you, @moshe , for the initiative in involving community members to discuss the SAPPHIRE key learning points. I've tried to set my understanding down. Would be happy to have feedback and improvise further. 

Businesses are turning to artificial intelligence more and more as the digital transformation path speeds at an unheard-of rate to release fresh degrees of efficiency, insight, and creativity. SAP presented its audacious vision for the future of intelligent business at SAP SAPPHIRE Orlando, based on the seamless integration of artificial intelligence, a uniform data foundation via the SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), and a strong commitment to sustainability. This seminar highlighted how SAP helps companies to fully utilize their data, including artificial intelligence right into main business operations, and satisfy rising social, environmental, and governance (ESG) needs. We will discuss the main lessons from SAPPHIRE 2025 in this blog, including SAP's advancements in artificial intelligence, BDC, the SAP Knowledge Graph, and sustainability solutions changing enterprise data management and producing smarter, more responsible business outcomes.

1.1 SAP Business Data Cloud: The Consolidated Foundation for Business Integration and Artificial Intelligence
SAP's artificial intelligence strategy revolves on the Business Data Cloud, an industrial-grade data mesh integrating data from SAP's vast portfolio and outside sources into a single, trusted semantic layer. From on-site systems, cloud apps, and outside partners to structured and unstructured data, BDC combines to provide accurate forecasting, simplified reporting, and AI-driven insights that support agile decision-making.

Refer to Data Products in SAP Business Data Cloud | SAP Architecture Center by Uma Anbazhagan, Jason Luo, James Rapp, Peter Schmidt

figure : This diagram illustrates how consumers discover, integrate, and access data products.

Scenarios analysis : 

Imagine a CFO who following a quarterly review must rapidly evaluate working capital and cash flow. Without waiting for hand-written reports, she accesses real-time financial insights, finds bottlenecks, and projects cash flow scenarios using BDC's prebuilt data products and dynamic dashboards. This shows how BDC turns unprocessable data into useful intelligence therefore enabling quicker, data-driven decisions.
Standardizing data across SAP systems including finance, supply chain, procurement, and HR into a uniform data model is one of BDC's strengths; another is integrating outside data for a complete corporate picture.

Strength:

• Directly supporting sophisticated analytics and machine learning on data with a business context.
• Using relationships with Adobe, Moody's, Thomson Reuters, and others to create intelligent applications atop BDC, hence enabling an open environment.

My Next learning focus was on 

1.2. How SAP Knowledge Graph Improves AI Understanding and Provides a Unified Data Foundation for Artificial Intelligence?

Embedded SAP Knowledge Graph, which serves as the semantic backbone linking data, metadata, and business processes, is fundamental component of BDC's power. Collectively, they provide a consistent, trustworthy, semantically enhanced data platform that enables artificial intelligence to provide accurate, context-aware insights and automation.
Building an AI Unified Data Foundation
Together with outside systems, BDC harmonizes data from SAP applications such S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba into a single, uniform semantic layer. Both organized and unstructured data are included in this consolidation to enable flawless integration all across the company.

Key features to explore: Standardizing several datasets into a cohesive model that maintains business meaning and linkages is known as semantic data harmony.
• Designed Data Products: Providing fast accelerating AI model development ready-to-use, premium datasets.
Native integration with SAP Dataphere and Databricks facilitates the whole AI lifecycle.
For reliable artificial intelligence, data quality, lineage, security, and compliance must all be guaranteed.
• Real-Time Adaptation: Let artificial intelligence programs dynamically automate tasks and learn constantly.
• Support for Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) by means of a single source of truth so strengthening AI agents such as SAP's Joule copilot.

You can further learn from 

a. Exploring SAP Knowledge Graph

b. Illustrating the Role of SAP Datasphere in SAP Business Data Clou

Figure: SAP Knowledge Graph enhances the integration and understanding of complex business data within SAP systems.

SAP's "Business AI" concept centers on immediately embedding artificial intelligence capabilities into key applications to automate regular chores and maintain human control. From 230 to a projected 400 by mid-2025, the number of embedded artificial intelligence scenarios is fast rising and spans domains including expense management, sourcing, work order creation, and employee self-service.

One of the most exciting reveals is SAP Sapphire Orlando Keynote: Bring Out Your Best | SAP News Center

2. The SAP Flywheel: AI, Data, and Applications Synergistically

SAP developed the "Flywheel" idea to show how apps create real-time business data that feeds embedded artificial intelligence to improve decisions and AI improves applications in turn. SAP's unified cloud suite and BDC's harmonic data foundation thus provide a compounding cycle of business value.
The SAP Flywheel shows the virtuous cycle whereby applications create real-time business data, embedded artificial intelligence uses this data to better decision-making, and AI improvements feed back into applications—creating constant, compounding business value.

The last not the least  Although I have been upskilling in multiple SAP business and technical area throughout the years, sustainability offers me motivation outside of the workplace. And here are the main points of interest on SAP sustainability.

3. SAP's Sustainability Emphasizing Intelligent Solutions to Drive ESG

SAP highlighted its increasing sustainability commitment at SAPPHIRE Orlando 2025, introducing fresh tools and data products meant to enable companies to reach social, environmental, and governance (ESG) targets. Deeply connected with the Business Data Cloud, SAP's sustainability solutions help businesses to gather, harmonize, and evaluate sustainability data in tandem with operational measurements.
Important advances in sustainability include:
• SAP Green Ledger: Incorporates environmental impact and carbon emissions tracking straight inside corporate transactions by including sustainability accounting into financial systems.

• SAP Product Footprint Management:  Calculations made utilizing current ERP data can help to embed footprint results back into company processes, therefore influencing decision-making. Driven by artificial intelligence, use supplier specific footprints and increase accuracy with emission factor mapping. computing a scale carbon footprint Calculate and control scope 1, 2, and 3 corporate, value chain, and product carbon footprints. Get a total, thorough understanding of your carbon footprint data from all over your value chain.

• Sustainability Control Tower: An intelligent tool driven by BDC that offers real-time ESG metric visibility, therefore enabling companies to track regulatory compliance and development toward sustainability targets.

All available within the one data fabric, sustainability data products are curated datasets encompassing environment, health, and safety (EHS) compliance, carbon footprint, and social responsibility metrics.

I would encourage more people to explore SAP sustainability products and how business processes and technical areas can be improvised to incorporate sustainability from the very start of the business.

Further, you can learn from, 

Explaining key features of SAP Green Ledger 

Administering Sustainability Control Tower | SAP Learning

Sustainable operations with AI powered SAP Environment, Health and Safety (EHS) - YouTube

4. Conclusion
SAP SAPPHIRE Orlando 2025 made abundantly evident that data-driven, highly integrated with sustainability at its center, artificial intelligence-powered enterprise technology is what will shape business technologies going forward. Enhanced by the SAP Knowledge Graph and supported by intelligent sustainability solutions, the SAP Business Data Cloud forms a strong semantic basis that lets artificial intelligence really grasp intricate business data and linkages while allowing companies to fulfill their ESG obligations. This basis is changing how companies run, make decisions, develop, and help to create a more sustainable future along with SAP's growing array of embedded AI applications and intelligent automation technologies. SAP's consistent approach presents a convincing roadmap to unlock new business value, encourage agility, and create a resilient, responsible, and intelligent corporation fit for the challenges of tomorrow as companies get ready for the next wave of digital transformation.

Would request @KunalBansal , @sayontan098  to share their learnings. 

Thank you once again..

entry.title: Re: How to move amount when update new GL in SAC
entry.content:
@Krisna2101 

You can use a Copy Data Action to copy from Old Account to New Account and later remove the amount from old account. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/a27d8405ac9b4e7bb8e50e8e70ba18a2.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=release&q=Copy%20Data%20Action#adding-a-copy-step

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAC roles/teams issue
entry.content: You can try removing all roles and assign back one by one and see which role is giving them access to view all data.
